---
title: "Slide Title"
subtitle: "Slide Subtitle"
author: "Author Name"
date: "2026-03-17"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: slides.scss
    transition: fade
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: true
footer: "DSFC Slides"
editor: source
execute:
  freeze: auto
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

# Add any setup code here (e.g., library calls, data loading)
```

# Section Title {background-color="#4C326A"}

## Slide with bullet points

- First point
- Second point
- Third point

## Slide with incremental bullets

::: {.incremental}
- This appears first
- Then this
- And finally this
:::

## Slide with code

```{r}
#| label: example-code
#| echo: true
#| eval: false

# Example R code
library(tidyverse)

data <- tibble(
  x = 1:10,
  y = rnorm(10)
)

ggplot(data, aes(x, y)) +
  geom_point()
```

## Slide with a task box

::: {.task}
**Your turn!**

1. Do this first thing
2. Then do this second thing
3. Finally do this third thing
:::

## Slide with highlighted text

This is normal text with [highlighted in peach]{.highlight-peach} and [highlighted in yellow]{.highlight-yellow} inline.

## Slide with handwritten text

::: {.hand-purple-large}
Key takeaway!
:::

## Slide with columns

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Left column**

- Point A
- Point B
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Right column**

- Point C
- Point D
:::

::::

## Slide with smaller text {.smaller}

This slide uses smaller text throughout. Useful for slides with more content that needs to fit.

## Slide with an image

![Image caption](path/to/image.png){fig-align="center" width="80%"}

# Another Section {background-color="#4C326A"}

## Learn more

::: {.learn-more}
[https://example.com](https://example.com)
:::

## Thank you! {background-color="#4C326A"}

::: {.hand-purple-large}
Questions?
:::
